office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: The race contrasts a long-serving legislator emphasizing fiscal restraint and housing supply with a transit worker and labor advocate emphasizing public investment and worker supports.

candidates.summary: Barkis is a state representative and property-management business owner emphasizing restrained transportation budgeting and housing availability.
highlights: Supports fiscally restrained transportation budgets and housing availability [S1]
Reports legislative work on housing supply and transportation budgets [S1]

spending.basis: He explicitly supports fiscally restrained transportation budgets.

housing.basis: His documented housing work emphasizes increasing supply and availability.

experience.basis: He has served in the Legislature since 2016 and reports a legislative record on housing and transportation.

candidates.summary: Dehnel is a transit worker, union officer, and legislative advocate focused on wages, public supports, and infrastructure.
highlights: Supports wages, collective bargaining, affordable health care, and childcare [S1]
Supports rural communities, infrastructure, and restrictions on predatory pricing [S1]

spending.basis: He supports infrastructure and public supports including affordable health care and childcare.

experience.basis: He brings labor and legislative-advocacy experience but no documented elected record.
